title: Distributed lock for singleton job execution
scenario_context: Multiple instances run cron. Without lock, job runs N times.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Lock without TTL — deadlock on crash
reasoning: SET NX with TTL. Lock owner can unlock. TTL covers crashes.
frontend_solution:
// Frontend contract for e2e-211: render the user-visible state, drive it through real interactions, and assert accessible output.
// Prefer role/name selectors, wait for network-visible state changes, and cover loading/error/empty/success paths without relying on implementation details.

error_handling_strategy.client_error_mapping: Map each code to a visible retry, denied, empty, or validation state.
error_handling_strategy.user_facing_messages: Short actionable message with no raw stack/provider details.
error_handling_strategy.recovery_actions: Retry only idempotent operations; otherwise refresh state or request access.

edge_cases_addressed: TTL too short — job continues without lock
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) NX + TTL. 2) Owner-only release via Lua. 3) Crash-safe.

title: Adding webhook with delivery test
scenario_context: User adds webhook URL. Naive: save and hope. Better: test delivery before saving as active.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User saves URL with typo; never knows webhooks fail.
reasoning: On save, fire test event with signed payload. Show result. Only mark verified after success.
